assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
The recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised intragroup monetary item exposed to foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Risk component designation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits designation of only the changes in cash flows or fair value attributable to a specific risk as the hedged item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and their components.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a hedge accounting model and the context includes qualifying foreign currency hedges of forecast intragroup transactions in consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate foreign currency hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
